description: Pocket rectangle that rules your waking hours
Ring ring, a voice arrives from far away
Thumbs talk when voices feel too heavy
Frozen moment, captured and kept in your pocket
Moving pictures playing endlessly on tiny screens
Levels, lives, and one more round before bed
Earbuds in, the whole world gets a soundtrack
Little bubbles bouncing back and forth all day
Glowing glass where modern life actually happens
Tiny eye on the back of every pocket
One gentle finger press and things happen
Finger flick that decides dates and destinies
Sending the joy along so everyone sees it
A tiny thumb or heart that feeds the ego
Publishing your breakfast for the whole world
One button whoosh, no taking it back
Tucking it away before the machine forgets
Gone with one press, or so you hope
The answer you owe that waiting bubble
Little list hiding all the choices
Tiny picture that opens a whole world
Named bundle of bits waiting in a drawer
Yellow paper pocket, reborn on every desktop
One long stretch of the endless web
The world wide something, spun without spiders
Where every dot com address finally lands
Squeaky little rodent turned desk companion
Rows of letters clacking under busy fingers
Office beast, always hungry, always jammed
Bigger than a pocket, smaller than a desk
Little box that fills the room with sound
The bar that drains right when you need it
The cord everyone borrows and never returns
Tangled spaghetti behind every desk
Up and down until the neighbors complain
Silence with one press, meetings love it
Those little bars you beg for on road trips
The front door ritual of every account
Your curated self, smiling on the internet
Added with one click, sometimes never met
Trailing a stranger's life, one update at a time
The digital door slammed politely shut
Face or fingerprint, then everything opens
Pulling treasure from the cloud to your pocket
Shiny new toy with buttons and a battery
The nagging popup promising fixes and surprises
Have you tried turning it off and on
Fresh spin-up after the machine's little nap
Little envelope of words landing with a ding
The tune that makes strangers check their pockets
Nine more minutes, the morning's sweetest lie
Ears covered, microphone ready, squad waiting
The tower and monitor throne of home computing
The big glowing window your computer stares through
Invisible threads tying every machine together
Signals through thin air, no cords attached
The handle you picked at thirteen and regret
Short line announcing your mood to everybody
The tiny face standing in for you everywhere
Endless river of posts you cannot stop stirring
Here at breakfast, vanished by tomorrow's dinner
A little magic that erases every blemish
Cartoon reaction slapped into the conversation
The witty line beneath the picture
Where strangers argue beneath every post
Naming your friends so the photo finds them
What everyone suddenly cares about this week
Subscribe, ring the bell, watch the uploads roll
Songs queued for the drive, the gym, the mood
A whole library sleeping inside one slim slab
Uninvited window shouting about prizes you never won
The spare copy you praise only after disaster
Change it here, it changes everywhere at once
The sneeze your computer catches from shady corners
Too good to be true, and it was
Breaking in with keystrokes instead of crowbars
Vanishing mid-conversation, leaving messages on read
The moment reality flickers inside the machine
The fatal pause between your click and the action
A tiny hello across the wire, then an echo
Lines of strange symbols making machines obey
Fueled by coffee, speaking fluent machine
One tiny square of light among millions
Thumbs of steel, midnight snacks, one more match
Miss one day and the little flame dies
The ending, blurted before you got there
Daily life, narrated straight into a lens
Little live tile living on your home screen
Slicing the tape on camera for millions
Buzzing camera hovering where you cannot climb
Hunting the gremlin hiding in your program
Small program doing the boring stuff for you
Bolt-on extra teaching old software new tricks
The dot com real estate everyone squats on
Renting a distant machine to keep your pages up
Vast organized vault of rows and records
Black window, blinking prompt, pure keyboard power
Living room box built for controllers and quests
Wobbling lever steering pixels since the arcade era
Old classics reborn on hardware they never knew
Live on camera, chasing subs and donations
Answers instantly, suspiciously polite, never sleeps
Fresh arrival still learning where the buttons live
Little arrow lifting good posts toward glory
A familiar face saying words it never spoke
Digital coins riding a rollercoaster of hype
The original digital gold, mined by machines
The article ends right where your money begins
Slides, a shaky mic, and a hundred muted squares
The office's private little corner, closed to outsiders
The middleman passing your requests along quietly
Light itself sprinting through hair-thin glass
The trusty blue cable when airwaves misbehave
The gift horse galloping through your inbox
Free software that pays itself in popups
Millions of passwords spilled in one dark night
The crack in the armor, found and abused
Small fix stitched over the broken spot
Little proof you already knocked and entered
Safe little playground where nothing can break loose
Software soldered into the machine's very bones
Silly name, essential nub hanging from the port
Pen that never touches paper, only glass
Fingers glide, the pointer obeys, no rodent required
The little brains coordinating everything on the board
The strict translator turning your words into machine speak
Saving your work into history with a message
Two branches of work becoming one, fingers crossed
The kitchen of the app, hidden from diners
The pretty face users actually get to touch
Scrambled letters keeping secrets from prying eyes
Sweet fake target waiting for greedy intruders
Borrowed identity, forged address, perfect disguise
Ruining the raid purely for the giggles
Random sprinkle making every stored password unique
One-way scramble, same input, same strange fingerprint
Quick math proving the download arrived intact
The extra bit that tattles when one flips
Half a byte, cuter than it sounds
Squeezing the picture, quietly discarding detail forever
A picture told strictly in rows of dots
Scattered dots faking colors the palette lacks
A pixel's blocky cousin living in three dimensions
Greek letter beloved by functional programmers everywhere
Burrowing menus through the internet before the web won
Whistling exact tones to fool the telephone company
Cracked software traded in shadowy corners
Cryptic symbols matching patterns, unreadable next morning
Sprawling newsgroups where flame wars were invented
Command line bloodhound sniffing lines for patterns
